browserNameSearchTab: Gap-filled Fractional Snow Cover, Europe, Daily, 60m, (2016-present), V1
browserSearchPath: CLMS Bio-geophysical Parameters, Snow, Snow Cover Extent
collectionDescription:
The daily cumulative **Gap-filled Fractional Snow Cover (GFSC)** product provides pixel-wise snow cover extent as a percentage (0% – 100%). It is based on synthetic aperture radar (SAR) data from the **Sentinel-1** constellation and optical imagery from the **Sentinel-2** constellation. The product is generated by merging all available Sentinel-1 and Sentinel-2 observations acquired over the previous **seven days** to produce a spatially complete composite of snow conditions, thereby reducing data gaps caused by cloud cover and sensor coverage limitations. Snow information derived from Sentinel-1 is focused on the detection of wet snow in high-mountain areas.

It is generated on a daily basis at European scale, with a spatial resolution of **60 m x 60 m**.

**Data availability**: from 2016 to the present.

More information [here](https://land.copernicus.eu/en/products/snow/high-resolution-gap-filled-fractional-snow-cover).

layers.longDescription: Fraction of the surface covered by snow (ranging from 0% to 100%). The dataset has also the following classes: cloud or cloud shadow (205), inland water (210), and no data (255).

layers.shortDescription: Quality layer for the snow fraction (GF) layer
layers.longDescription: Quality layer providing basic assessment of the snow fraction GF layer. For pixels with a QAFLAGS bit 7 value of 0, the data quality refers to the Sentinel-2 optical snow data quality. For pixels with a QAFLAGS bit 7 value of 1, the data quality refers to the Sentinel-1 SAR wet snow data quality.

Bitmask layer with pixel quality flags. The default visualisation displays the sensor type of the satellite data (optical=0 in blue, radar=1 in pink). These correspond to pixels where bit 7 is activated. Additional information can also be visualised by selecting other bits. More details are available in the </> Custom option.

layers.shortDescription: Sensing start date (seconds from Unix time).
layers.longDescription: Timestamp of the data acquisition date. Expressed as the total number of seconds elapsed since the Unix epoch (01 January 1970, 00:00:00 UTC).
